content:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

There are frontend bugs, and then there are haunted frontend bugs.

2 min read
1 hour ago

--

This one looked suspicious at first. We had just added a shiny little <relative-time> component to the AG Grid dataset list. It rendered dates more nicely, it felt cleaner, and then suddenly the console lit up with errors.
Thirty-seven of them.
Naturally, <relative-time> got side-eyed immediately.
The error was:
Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'offsetHeight')It came from MDUI’s tooltip internals, specifically inside updatePositioner().
At first glance, that seemed odd. There was no mdui-tooltip in the date column.
But there was an action menu.
And that action menu used mdui-button-icon.
The plot twist: mdui-button-icon contains an internal tooltip.
So even though we never wrote a tooltip directly, one was quietly living inside the button.
AG Grid virtualizes rows for performance. When you scroll, it destroys and recycles DOM nodes very quickly. MDUI’s tooltip, meanwhile, uses async positioning logic through things like requestAnimationFrame, observers, and layout measurements.
That creates a tiny race condition.
AG Grid says:
This row is gone now.
MDUI says, slightly later:
Cool, let me measure that button’s height.
Except the button is already gone.
So MDUI reaches for offsetHeight, but the element has vanished.
That is the bug.
To make sure <relative-time> was not the culprit, we added a quick log inside the date-rendering requestAnimationFrame:
console.log("[relative-time rAF] isConnected:", el.isConnected, "date:", date);The result?
[relative-time rAF] isConnected: true
[relative-time rAF] isConnected: true
[relative-time rAF] isConnected: trueEvery date element was still connected when it updated.
Then the tooltip crashed anyway.
That tells us the issue is not the date component. The crash is coming from the action column’s mdui-button-icon, whose internal tooltip is trying to reposition after AG Grid has already removed the row.
So the bug was pre-existing.
<relative-time> did not introduce it. It merely happened to be nearby when the ghost appeared.
The lesson: not every new component near a stack trace is guilty. Sometimes the real culprit is an invisible tooltip hiding inside a button, waiting for virtualization to make things weird.
The likely fix is to guard tooltip/action rendering during row teardown, avoid tooltips inside virtualized cells, or replace mdui-button-icon in AG Grid action cells with a simpler icon button that does not attach async tooltip behavior.
In short:
relative-time: innocent
AG Grid virtualization: fast
MDUI tooltip: too trusting
offsetHeight: attempted on a ghost
